title: Python Data Types, Lists & Functions Explained | Tutorial 2 of 10
description:
The type error that makes your analysis wrong without crashing — and the four Python structures that prevent it.

In Tutorial 1 we built our first script. In Tutorial 2 we go one layer deeper: what Python actually stores, how types behave differently, and the structures every data science pipeline depends on before pandas ever enters the picture.

What you will learn: the four primitive types (int, float, str, bool) and the silent bugs each one hides, how to check and convert types before any calculation, lists and dicts and the difference that matters in real data work, list comprehensions as one-line filters and transforms, and functions — the habit that makes your code trustworthy.

[TIMESTAMPS_PLACEHOLDER]

This is part of a 10-tutorial Python for Data Science series built for working professionals. Practical from the start, no fluff.
